# blends

The objective of blends is to blend colour palettes using blend modes,
such as multiply and screen.

## Installation

Install from CRAN, or development version from
[GitHub](https://github.com/).

``` r
install.packages("blends") 
pak::pak("davidhodge931/blends")
```

## Example

``` r
library(blends)
library(jumble)
scales::show_col(c(teal, orange, multiply(teal, orange)), ncol = 3)
```

![](reference/figures/README-setup-1.png)

![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-3-1.png)![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-4-1.png)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`colour_burn()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/colour_burn.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using colour burn mode
- [`colour_dodge()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/colour_dodge.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using colour dodge mode
- [`darken()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/darken.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using darken mode
- [`difference()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/difference.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using difference mode
- [`exclusion()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/exclusion.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using exclusion mode
- [`hard_light()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/hard_light.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using hard light mode
- [`lighten()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/lighten.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using lighten mode
- [`multiply()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/multiply.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using multiply mode
- [`overlay()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/overlay.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using overlay mode
- [`screen()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/screen.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using screen mode
- [`soft_light()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/blends/reference/soft_light.md)
  : Blend colours and palettes using soft light mode
